mappings.rationale: Directly covers the art of theatre, theatrical production, staging, acting, directing, and theatre history — the core subject of this entire section.
Directly covers tragedy as a traditional dramatic form, explicitly listed under kinds of theatrical production in section B.1.a.i.
Directly covers comedy as a traditional dramatic form, explicitly listed under kinds of theatrical production in section B.1.a.i.
Covers modern theatrical production and dramatic forms, relevant to the section's treatment of Western theatre history and contemporary staging.
Shakespeare is central to Western theatre history, and his works exemplify dramatist-controlled productions and period styles discussed in this section.
Covers Shakespearean comedy in performance, relevant to the section's treatment of comedy as theatrical genre and Western theatre history.
Covers Shakespearean tragedy in performance, relevant to the section's treatment of tragedy as theatrical genre and Western theatre history.
Shaw is a major figure in modern Western theatre, relevant to the section's treatment of Western theatre history and dramatist-controlled productions.
Sophocles is foundational to Western theatre and tragedy, relevant to the section's treatment of ancient Greek theatre and Western theatre history.
Relevant to the section's treatment of theatre buildings, stages, and auditoriums as production areas (section C.1).
Relevant to the section's treatment of staging and stage design, including arrangement of sets, lighting, and costumes for theatrical effect.
Relevant to the section's treatment of comedy, farce, music hall, variety, and burlesque productions.
Covers ancient Greek and Roman dramatic literature, relevant to the origins of Western theatre discussed in section D.
Ancient Greek and Roman theatre traditions are the foundation of Western theatre history covered in this section.
Relevant to the section's treatment of the origins of theatre, religious theatre, and the relationship between ritual and theatrical performance.
Relevant to the section's treatment of costume design as an element of theatrical production (section C.2).
Music is one of the arts that plays a role in theatre, relevant to the section's discussion of the roles of other arts in the theatre (section A.8).
Camus was a significant playwright and theatre figure, relevant to the section's treatment of Western theatre history.
Relevant to the creative process of theatrical production, acting, and the audience's engagement with theatrical performance.
